posts.title: IMO Net-Zero Framework Could Generate $11-12 Billion in Revenues
posts.excerpt: The IMO's Net-Zero Framework could generate $11-12 billion annually, but its governance and revenue distribution remain unclear. Without safeguards, funding may favor nations with established project pipelines, undermining a fair transition. Delaying the vote until later this year raises concerns of a potential watering down of the framework due to opposition.
posts.author: DirtiestShips

posts.title: Decarbonization of the Maritime Industry: CMA CGM and SIPG Energy Complete CMA CGM’s First Biomethanol Bunkering
posts.excerpt: CMA CGM bunkered 3,643 tonnes of biomethanol in Shanghai, but dirtiestships.com asks: what is the well-to-wake carbon intensity? This single bunkering does not negate CMA CGM's extensive use of HFO across their fleet. Will *CMA CGM OSMIUM's* CII rating exceed its design efficiency, triggering FuelEU Maritime penalties?
posts.author: DirtiestShips

posts.title: LNG and methanol create path dependencies that divert investments from shipping’s long-term decarbonisation pathway, new study warns
posts.excerpt: Another "study" claims that LNG and methanol ships risk creating stranded assets, further entrenching fossil fuel dependency. The research suggests these fuels fail as "stepping stones" to zero-emission shipping, diverting investment from genuine decarbonization. Will shipowners ever learn that burning fossil fuels, even "less dirty" ones, will lead to FuelEU Maritime penalties?
posts.author: DirtiestShips

posts.title: Panama's Carbon Levy Backstab: A Major Blow to Shipping Decarbonization
posts.excerpt: The already fragile global consensus on shipping decarbonization has suffered a potentially fatal blow. posts.excerpt: RFOcean, a fully integrated European shipping company, has signed a binding long-term off-take agreement with ETFuels to purchase e-methanol at a fixed price from 2030.
